title: Executive Order 10816—Amendment of Executive Order No. 10501 of November 5, 1953, Relating to Safeguarding Official Information in the Interests of the Defense of the United States

flags.justification: The historical-research access provision expands rather than restricts access, and the discretion is exercised by each agency over its own originated material — narrow, self-accountable, liberalizing discretion.

Agency heads granting historical-research access must 'take appropriate steps to assure that classified information is not published or otherwise compromised' — a real, if general, responsibility requirement.
Three narrow, related amendments (declassification clarification, historical-research access, mail-transmission rules) all coherently amend one underlying classification framework.

'Trustworthy' and 'clearly consistent with the interests of national defense' are subjective standards for the historical-research exception, even though the context is access-expanding rather than access-denying.
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.
Standing-procedural-framework category applies: this amends an ongoing classification system, itself evidence of periodic revision as needed.
This expands access beyond the prior restrictive baseline; no oversight mechanism is removed.
This concerns internal agency discretion over who gets access, not an ongoing third-party compliance regime.
A narrow, beneficial amendment expanding historical-research access; no concerning broader claim.
No second agency or mandate is implicated.

The historical-research exception requires an affirmative dual determination (national-defense consistency and trustworthiness) plus ongoing agency responsibility — narrow and conditioned, not a broad undermining carve-out.
